p.strong: Performance Impact of Hardware Prefetcher Alignment

div.p: Hardware prefetchers (specifically the Stream Prefetcher) are trained on virtual addresses. A stride of exactly 64 bytes (1 cache line) is a high-confidence signal to the CPU to fetch addr + 64, addr + 128, ... into the L2/L1 cache before the program requests them.

When the stride is 65, the access address crosses the 64-byte boundary differently for every access. This jitter prevents the prefetcher from locking onto a stream, resulting in constant Cache Misses and forcing the CPU to wait for main memory (DRAM) on nearly every instruction.

g.text: void run_stride_test(char* data, size_t len, size_t stride) {
